source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
JOB DESCRIPTION

At Vori, Forward Deployed Data Engineers bridge the gap between customer systems and our modern grocery platform. You’ll work closely with onboarding managers to configure Vori for each retailer’s unique needs—reducing out-of-stocks, cutting shrink, and driving efficiency.

As one of the Founding Forward Deployed Data Engineers, you won’t just be implementing processes—you’ll be defining them from the ground up. You’ll create the playbooks, data pipelines, and integration systems that allow Vori to scale from our first customers to thousands of stores.

This role is hands-on with data. You’ll wrangle item files and invoices from POS systems, back-office tools, and ERPs—in formats ranging from CSV and EDI to PDFs and even scanned paper—and connect them seamlessly into Vori. Fluency with SQL and data manipulation is essential.

We’re looking for motivated, detail-oriented engineers who thrive at the intersection of people, data, and infrastructure. If you love solving messy integration problems, collaborating with customers, and making technology work in the real world, you’ll feel right at home at Vori.


RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Understand the VoriOS system end to end and interdependencies between the system and its integration components

 - Understanding Customer’s needs to define and configure system specifications

 - Configure and maintain data mappings and reconciliation as an integral part of our operations team

 - Ability to thrive in a cross-functional environment and work closely with Operations, Product, Engineering teams on how to improve workflows and systems end to end


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years in FD/Solutions Engineering, or a similar customer-facing technical role, ideally in B2B SaaS or fintech

 - Hybrid skillset — strong backend/data engineering fundamentals + ability to interact directly with customer IT teams.

 - Domain awareness — familiarity with unstructured data, ETL processes and ML/OCR pipelines.

 - Customer empathy — comfort in troubleshooting messy, high-pressure customer environments.

 - Bias for autonomy — capable of managing extraction, ingestion, and pipeline tuning with minimal guidance.

 - SQL, Regex and scripting proficiency and willingness to learn new technologies.


GROWTH OPPORTUNITIES

 - Get excited to learn more about complex data systems on the tech side as well as business challenges the grocery industry faces everyday

 - Confront and conquer unique, real-world problems that enhance your problem-solving abilities and challenge you to perform well with high velocity


EXPECTATIONS

 - You will be stretched and challenged with a large scope of work, and fast paced deadlines. This role is for folks that want to make a dent in a $1T industry — which requires consistent force of will and large doses of daily creativity

 - Vori is NOT a simple 9-5 job 💯. We are building a Seal Team 6 of individuals who work hard, long, and smart to deliver exceptional results on limited resources. As a startup we constantly have to prove our right to exist. But if we win, we have the chance to transform the most fundamental industry on earth — food. Are you in?


WHY VORI

 - Mission-driven team building tech that helps local businesses thrive

 - Backed by top investors and advisors from Stripe, Uber, Palantir, and Toast

 - Real revenue, real growth, and a massive market opportunity

 - A chance to shape not just marketing—but the company's future

source_jobs.title: Territory Account Executive - PNW (WA/ORE/ID/MT)
source_jobs.description:
Location: Pacific Northwest (Remote / Field-Based)
Department: Sales – Grocery & Retail Vertical
Reports To: Regional POD Lead






ABOUT VORI

Vori is transforming the future of the $1T grocery industry through an integrated operating system designed for independent and regional grocers.

We’re a rapidly growing fintech + vertical AI startup backed by top investors, providing cutting-edge point of sale (POS), payment processing, and inventory management solutions that empower retailers to thrive in a world dominated by retail giants like Walmart and Amazon.

Our mission is simple but bold — reduce food waste, improve food access, and strengthen local communities. By connecting retailers, distributors, and brands through technology, we’re helping grocers operate smarter, faster, and more sustainably.






THE ROLE

We’re looking for a driven, entrepreneurial Territory Account Executive (TAE) to help us build out new markets and bring an entire industry online for the first time.

As a TAE, you’ll be the face of Vori in the field — pulling doors, meeting store owners, and running the full sales cycle from prospecting to close. You’ll partner directly with independent grocery operators and local market leaders to show them how Vori’s AI-driven platform can modernize their operations, improve margins, and future-proof their business.

This is an exciting opportunity to join a fast-paced, high-impact sales team where every deal you close directly helps a small business compete and thrive.






WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Prospect and open new business: Pull doors, make cold calls, and conduct in-person meetings with grocery owners, managers, and operators in your assigned territory.

 - Run the full sales cycle: From lead generation to demo, proposal, negotiation, and close — you’ll own your pipeline end-to-end.

 - Build and manage territory plans: Use HubSpot CRM to track activity, forecast results, and report on KPIs.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams: Work closely with Product, Marketing, and Customer Success to ensure smooth onboarding and long-term success.

 - Be a market expert: Understand local grocery dynamics, competition, and trends to help shape Vori’s go-to-market strategy.

 - Represent the brand: Be the trusted local face of Vori, embodying our mission to empower community grocers through technology.






WHAT YOU BRING

 - Experience in field sales or business development, ideally selling technology, POS systems, payments, or services to grocery or retail businesses.

 - A strong track record of meeting or exceeding quota in a full-cycle sales environment.

 - Proven ability to cold call, generate leads, and close deals with limited supervision.

 - Entrepreneurial mindset — self-starter who thrives in fast-paced, ambiguous, startup environments.

 - Exceptional communication, relationship-building, and presentation skills.

 - Tech-savvy and organized, with proficiency in CRM tools like HubSpot (preferred).

 - Background in or passion for the grocery industry is a major plus (experience as a cashier, buyer, store director, IT lead, or vendor is highly valued).

 - Minimal travel required; majority of activity will be within your local territory.






BONUS POINTS FOR

 - Experience selling in a high-growth startup environment.

 - Prior exposure to AI-driven SaaS or fintech platforms.

 - Familiarity with independent grocery or CPG ecosystems.




WHY JOIN VORI

 - Be part of a mission-driven startup tackling one of the most essential and under-served industries in America.

 - Equity ownership – participate in the upside as we grow.

 - Collaborative, fast-paced culture that rewards curiosity, initiative, and results.

 - Opportunity to build your territory from the ground up and shape the future of Vori’s go-to-market motion.




ACTIVE TERRITORIES

We are actively hiring Territory Account Executives in the following markets:

 - Seattle, WA

 - Portland, OR

 - Boise, ID

 - Missoula, MT

Each region re

source_jobs.title: Growth Marketing Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT VORI:

Vori is transforming the future of the $1T grocery industry. Vori is a rapidly growing fintech + vertical AI startup that provides an integrated operating system  POS, payment processing, and inventory management. We help local and regional grocery businesses thrive and compete against retail giants like Walmart and Amazon. Our mission is to reduce food waste and food insecurity, nourishing communities across the country.



POSITION OVERVIEW:

We are seeking an exceptional Growth Lead to help drive Vori's revenue by 10x over the next year. This role will be pivotal in scaling our customer acquisition engine. The ideal candidate will be a world-class expert in B2B SaaS growth marketing, with a proven track record of driving qualified leads and converting them into revenue.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Develop and execute a comprehensive demand generation strategy to achieve aggressive growth targets

 - Drive a culture of experimentation, learning, and velocity

 - Own the full-funnel metrics, from top-of-funnel lead generation to sales- qualified opportunities

 - Optimize the marketing tech stack to enable efficient growth Implement data-driven, multi-channel marketing campaigns

 - Create compelling content and messaging that resonates with our target audience of independent grocers

 - Collaborate closely with sales, product, and customer success teams to align growth initiatives with overall business objectives


QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Experience marketing to SMB or enterprise customers in traditional industries (e.g., retail, logistics, supply chain, food, health, construction, trades)

 - 6+ years of experience in growth/demand gen in B2B SaaS marketing

 - Fluency in modern martech stacks and technical proficiency in marketing operations

 - Proven track record of scaling revenue in a fast paced environment

 - Strong analytical skills with the ability to use data to inform strategy and demonstrate ROI

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams and distill complexity into poetry

 - Strategic thinker with the ability to execute tactically in a fast-paced environment

 - Exceptional communication and presentation skills


IDEAL CANDIDATE QUALITIES:

 - Entrepreneurial: Comfortable with ambiguity and able to pivot strategies as needed. Prior startup exposure Series A  C or ex-founder experience is preferred

 - Bias Toward Experimentation: Thrives on testing new hypotheses, evaluating data, and iterating quickly

 - High Craft: Vori is a design-driven company, and we see taste as a core part of growth and demand generation

 - Results-driven: Consistently delivers on aggressive growth targets

 - Customer-obsessed: Deeply understands the needs and challenges of independent grocers

 - Growth Marketing Lead 2

 - Collaborative: Builds strong relationships across all departments, especially design, sales, and product


WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Opportunity to make a name for yourself, by owning a critical function in a fast growing company

 - Competitive compensation package including equity Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance 401(k) plan
   Flexible work arrangements

 - Professional development opportunities

 - As an early-stage company, we are still building out our growth engine, you should be excited about the opportunity to get in on the ground floor. If you're passionate about growth and excited about revolutionizing the grocery industry, we want to hear from you.

How to apply:

If you'd like to be considered, please reach out to us with a summary of your work and why you're excited to join Vori.

Email: careers@vori.com

source_jobs.title: Territory Account Executive-New York
source_jobs.description:
About Vori:

Vori is transforming the future of the $1T grocery industry. Vori is a rapidly growing fintech + vertical AI startup that provides an integrated operating system to local and regional grocery stores  POS, payment processing, and inventory management. We help retailers thrive and compete against retail giants like Walmart and Amazon. Our mission is to reduce food waste and food insecurity, nourishing communities across the country.

We're looking for a passionate, top-performing Territory Account Executive who's excited to bring an entire industry online for the first time. source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT REPRESENTATIVE (BDR) – PACIFIC NORTHWEST

Location: Remote / Field-Based (WA / OR / ID / MT)
Department: Sales – Grocery & Retail Vertical
Reports To: Regional POD Lead




ABOUT VORI

Vori is transforming the future of the $1T grocery industry through an integrated operating system designed for independent and regional grocers.

We’re a rapidly growing fintech + vertical AI startup backed by top investors, providing cutting-edge point of sale (POS), payment processing, and inventory management solutions that empower local retailers to thrive in a world dominated by retail giants like Walmart and Amazon.

Our mission is simple but bold — reduce food waste, improve food access, and strengthen local communities. By connecting retailers, distributors, and brands through technology, we’re helping grocers operate smarter, faster, and more sustainably.




THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Business Development Representative (BDR) to help us grow our footprint across the Pacific Northwest.

As a BDR at Vori, you’ll be responsible for building pipeline and opening doors for our Territory Account Executives (TAEs). You’ll prospect new grocery and specialty market accounts, research ownership groups, and connect with decision-makers to educate them on how Vori’s AI-driven platform can simplify operations and increase profitability.

This role is ideal for someone hungry to break into technology sales, ready to learn full-cycle selling, and motivated by seeing their outreach turn into real, measurable impact for small businesses.






WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Generate new opportunities daily: Cold call, email, and use social channels to connect with independent grocery stores, markets, and distributors.
   

 - Build the top of the funnel: Identify high-potential prospects through territory research, LinkedIn, Google Maps, and local outreach.
   

 - Book qualified meetings: Schedule and coordinate introductory calls and demos between grocery operators and our Territory Account Executives.
   

 - Collaborate with your TAE: Strategize weekly on territory plans, local events, and creative outreach campaigns.
   

 - Maintain pipeline hygiene: Track activity and engagement in HubSpot CRM to ensure data accuracy and pipeline visibility.
   

 - Experiment and iterate: Share learnings and feedback with leadership and product teams to continuously improve outreach messaging and targeting.
   






WHAT YOU BRING

 - 1–2 years of experience in sales, business development, or customer-facing roles (internships count).
   

 - Proven ability to cold call, build rapport, and handle objections with confidence.
   

 - Strong organizational skills and comfort managing multiple priorities.
   

 - A self-starter mentality — you’re scrappy, curious, and energized by building something new.
   

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
   

 - Familiarity with HubSpot or similar CRM tools preferred.
   

 - Passion for technology, local business, or the grocery/retail ecosystem a big plus.
   
   
   


BONUS POINTS FOR

 - Experience in a startup or fast-growing environment.
   

 - Previous exposure to POS, payments, SaaS, or fintech solutions.
   

 - Grocery or retail background (worked in or around the industry).
   




WHY JOIN VORI

 - Competitive base + uncapped commission.
   

 - Pre-IPO equity at a Series B, venture-backed company.
   

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision benefits.
   

 - Rapid growth and clear career path — top performers are promoted to Territory Account Executive roles.
   

 - A mission-driven culture focused on community, sustainability, and innovation.
   
   
   


ACTIVE TERRITORIES

We’re currently hiring BDRs to support our expansion in:

 - Seattle, WA
   

 - Portland, OR
   

 - Boise, ID
   

 - Missoula, MT
   

Each territory represents a key growth market for Vori’s grocery and retail strategy.

source_jobs.title: Customer Support Representative
source_jobs.description:
As a Support Representative, you will be on the front lines helping grocery store customers resolve issues related to our iOS-based POS software and hardware. This role focuses on troubleshooting day-to-day operational issues, guiding customers through workflows, and ensuring minimal disruption to in-store checkout. You will work closely with onboarding, installers, and advanced support teams to deliver a consistent and high-quality customer experience.

Key Responsibilities:

 - Provide phone, chat, and ticket-based support for grocery stores using our iOS POS system

 - Troubleshoot issues related to iPad POS terminals, payment devices, receipt printers, barcode scanners, scales (if applicable), and cash drawers

 - Support core POS workflows including checkout, scanning, payments, refunds, drawer balancing, inventory updates, and reporting

 - Assist customers with basic iOS device settings, connectivity issues, and peripheral pairing

 - Diagnose and resolve common software issues within the POS app and back-office tools

 - Clearly document issues, steps taken, and resolutions within the support ticketing
   
   system

 - Escalate complex or unresolved issues to higher-level support or engineering teams with
   
   proper context

 - Collaborate with onboarding and installation teams to support newly live stores

 - Identify recurring issues and provide feedback to product and support leadership

 - Maintain a calm, professional demeanor when supporting customers in high-pressure retail environments

Qualifications & Skills:

 - Required:
   
   - 1–3 years of experience in technical support, customer support, or retail
     
     technology support
   
   - Familiarity with iOS devices (iPads, basic iOS settings, Bluetooth/WiFi
     
     troubleshooting)
   
   - Experience supporting POS systems or retail software
   
   - Basic understanding of grocery store front-end operations
   
   - Strong problem-solving and troubleshooting skills
   
   - Excellent verbal and written communication skills
   
   - Ability to work scheduled shifts, including evenings or weekends if required
   
   * Preferred:
   
   - Experience supporting grocery, retail, or hospitality POS systems
   
   - Familiarity with payment processing and EMV devices
   
   - Experience using ticketing systems such as Zendesk, Freshdesk, or similar
   
   - Comfort working in a fast-paced, high-volume support environment

source_jobs.title: POS Installer
source_jobs.description:
As a POS Installer, you will be responsible for the physical installation, configuration, and validation of iOS-based POS systems in grocery store environments. This is a hands-on, customer-facing role focused on hardware setup, basic network connectivity, system testing, and ensuring stores are fully operational at go-live. You will work closely with our onboarding, support, and project management teams to deliver consistent, high-quality installations.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Install and configure iOS-based POS hardware in grocery store environments.

 - Set up and test iOS POS terminals, stands, payment devices, receipt printers, barcode scanners, scales (if applicable), and cash drawers

 - Mount hardware securely and ensure clean, professional cable management

 - Verify network connectivity (LAN/WiFi), device communication, and peripheral functionality

 - Perform end-to-end testing of checkout workflows, including scanning, payments, receipts, and drawer operations

 - Coordinate with internal onboarding and support teams to confirm readiness for go-live

 - Train store staff on basic hardware usage and operational best practices

 - Troubleshoot and resolve basic hardware, connectivity, or configuration issues during installation

 - Document installation details, issues encountered, and store sign-off upon completion.

 - Adhere to installation standards, safety guidelines, and customer experience expectations

 - Travel to customer sites as required, including early mornings, evenings, or weekends as needed
   
   

Qualifications & Skills:

 - Required:
   
   - 2+ years of experience installing POS systems,
   
   - IT equipment, or retail technology
   
   - Hands-on experience with iOS devices (iPads, iOS settings, device configuration)
   
   - Familiarity with grocery store environments and front-end checkout operations
   
   - Experience installing peripherals such as receipt printers, barcode scanners, cash drawers, and payment terminals
   
   - Basic networking knowledge (Ethernet, WiFi, IP addressing, connectivity testing)
   
   - Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills
   
   - Professional, customer-facing communication skills ○ Ability to lift and move equipment (up to ~50 lbs)
   
   - Willingness to travel extensively within assigned territory

 - Preferred:
   
   - Experience installing POS systems specifically for grocery stores
   
   - Familiarity with payment devices and EMV requirements
   
   - Experience with cable routing, countertop drilling, or light mounting work
   
   - Ability to read and follow installation diagrams and runbooks
   
   - Prior experience working with installation partners or field services teams

source_jobs.title: Onboarding Specialist
source_jobs.description:
As an Onboarding Specialist, you will be responsible for successfully implementing our IOS POS solution for newly acquired customers. You will coordinate the setup of POS hardware, configure the software, train users, and provide support to ensure a smooth transition. Your role is critical in setting customers up for long-term success with our platform.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Manage the end-to-end onboarding process for new customers, ensuring a seamless transition to our IOS-based POS system.

 - Configure and deploy POS software, including payment processing, inventory management, and financial reporting modules.

 - Oversee the setup and integration of hardware components, including IOS touch terminals, receipt printers, barcode scanners, and cash drawers.

 - Conduct remote and/or on-site training sessions for customers to ensure they are comfortable using the system.

 - Develop and maintain onboarding resources such as training guides, video tutorials, and FAQs.

 - Act as the primary point of contact for customers during the onboarding phase, addressing any questions or concerns.

 - Collaborate with sales, customer support, and development teams to ensure a smooth onboarding experience.

 - Identify and address potential challenges early in the onboarding process to minimize disruptions.

 - Track onboarding progress and report key milestones to internal stakeholders.

 - Provide feedback to product and support teams to improve the onboarding experience.



Qualifications & Skills:

Required:

 - 2+ years of experience in customer onboarding, technical support, or software implementation, preferably in the POS or fintech industry.

 - Familiarity with IOS OS and mobile device management.

 - Hands-on experience with POS hardware, including receipt printers, barcode scanners, and cash drawers.

 - Strong troubleshooting skills and the ability to guide customers through setup processes.

 - Excellent communication and customer service skills.

 - Ability to explain technical concepts in a clear and simple manner.

 - Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

 - Ability to work independently and manage multiple customer implementations simultaneously.

Preferred:

 - Experience with payment processing systems and POS software.

 - Knowledge of inventory management and financial reporting systems.

 - Familiarity with remote support tools and troubleshooting techniques.

 - Experience working in a SaaS or startup environment.

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Product Engineer - Agentic AI
source_jobs.description:
WHY THIS ROLE / WHY NOW

Software development is changing.

The traditional model where engineers manually write every feature is being replaced by agentic systems that design, generate, test, and ship software continuously.

At Vori, we’re not building traditional software.

We’re building the modern operating system for grocery, and the next phase of that platform is agentic infrastructure. Systems of AI agents that help retailers run their stores and help our own engineering teams ship faster.

Our long-term vision is the self-driving store:
an operating system where intelligent agents continuously optimize pricing, inventory, merchandising, and operations.

To build that future, we also need to transform how software itself is created.

That means evolving from a company that ships features to a company that orchestrates agents that ship features.

Every engineer at Vori is now an AI Product Engineer:

• Designing agent workflows
• Building context systems and knowledge layers
• Creating feedback loops that improve automatically
• Shipping products where AI is a core part of the system

You’ll work across product, design, and infrastructure to turn ideas into production AI systems that retailers rely on every day.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Design and ship agentic product systems, not just software features.

Partner with product managers, designers, and customers to identify problems where AI agents can meaningfully improve workflows.

Design and implement agent architectures including reasoning, planning, tool use, and feedback loops.

Build the infrastructure that powers AI systems including:

• orchestration frameworks
• context pipelines
• evaluation frameworks
• knowledge systems
• event-driven architectures

Work across the full stack from backend orchestration and data pipelines to user-facing interfaces that make AI systems understandable and trustworthy.

Ship fast prototypes, run experiments, and turn promising ideas into production systems used by retailers at scale.

Continuously integrate emerging AI capabilities and tools to increase both product intelligence and engineering velocity.

Improve how Vori builds software by helping the organization adopt agentic development workflows including AI-assisted coding, testing, debugging, and design.

Contribute to the evolution of Vori’s agentic development platform including infrastructure, tooling, and internal standards.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

You are an engineer who wants to build AI-native products, not bolt AI onto existing systems.

3–8+ years building production software systems.

Strong ability to build across the stack including backend services, data pipelines, and user-facing applications.

Experience working with or deep interest in technologies such as:

• LLM APIs
• agent frameworks and orchestration systems
• vector search and retrieval systems
• evaluation and feedback pipelines
• event-driven systems

You think in systems, not just endpoints.

You move quickly from idea → prototype → production.

You care deeply about customer outcomes, not just technical novelty.

You’re excited about shaping how teams build in an AI-native engineering organization.

You have a maker’s mindset: you thrive on prototyping, iterating quickly, and owning features from zero → one → scale.




WHY VORI

 - Mission-driven team building tech that helps local businesses thrive

 - Backed by top investors and advisors from Stripe, Uber, Palantir, and Toast

 - Real revenue, real growth, and a massive market opportunity

source_jobs.title: Product Support Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We are looking for a Product Support Engineer to serve as the technical bridge between our customers, support team, and engineering organization. This role focuses on diagnosing complex product issues, improving system reliability, and ensuring our support organization can resolve problems quickly and effectively.

Key Responsibilities:

 - Provide technical support and troubleshooting assistance to clients using our POS solutions.

 - Diagnose software, hardware, and network issues, working towards timely resolutions.

 - Act as a liaison between clients and the development team to communicate technical issues and feature requests.

 - Develop and maintain support documentation, FAQs, and knowledge base articles for customer self-service.

 - Assist in testing and validation of software updates, ensuring compatibility and performance.

 - Monitor system performance and provide recommendations for optimizations and enhancements.

 - Train clients and internal teams on new product features and best practices.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to improve product usability and overall customer experience.

 - Stay current with emerging technologies and industry trends to continuously improve support services.

Qualifications & Requirements:

 - 3+ years of experience in technical support, product support, or software engineering, preferably within the POS or retail technology industry.

 - Strong understanding of POS systems, payment processing, and network configurations.

 - Proficiency in troubleshooting software and hardware issues, including databases and APIs.

 - Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.

 - Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.

 - Experience with ticketing systems and remote troubleshooting tools.

source_jobs.title: VP, Finance
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT VORI

Vori is building modern technology for the grocery industry. The company serves independent and regional grocers with tools that help them run more efficient, data-driven businesses.




WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

Vori needs a finance executive who can own both sides of the finance mandate:

 1. External finance leadership — fundraising, debt financing, investor strategy, board narrative, and capital structure.

 2. Internal finance infrastructure — FP&A, cash planning, accounting foundation, reporting cadence, controls, and operating discipline.

This is not a ceremonial CFO role. It is a hands-on company-building role for someone who can operate at the executive level while still building the finance function from the ground up. We hire people with high agency and high urgency. We look for slope over y-intercept. We care less about where you trained and more about what you’ve built. At Vori, everyone is a builder who owns problems end to end and makes big decisions that shape real outcomes.




MISSION

The VP, Finance will help Vori make better strategic decisions, raise and allocate capital effectively, and build a finance function that gives the company reliable visibility into performance, runway, and tradeoffs.

The right person will be a trusted partner to the CEO and leadership team, a credible voice with investors and the board, and a builder who can turn messy operating reality into clear financial strategy.





WHO YOU ARE

You are a hands-on startup VP or CFO-caliber finance leader who has operated in high-growth, venture-backed environments. You have enough strategic finance depth to lead capital markets work and enough operating finance discipline to build the function yourself before the team is large.

You are likely to be a strong fit if you are a:

 - Builder: A proven builder who has created finance functions from scratch with your own hands, without a large team or mature systems. You also show adept, thoughtful, and creative use of AI in your financial craft.

 - Capital Markets Athlete: You have led or materially co-led fundraising with founders and executives. You command respect from capital partners and ensure Vori is an elite steward of other people’s money.

 - FP&A Master: exceptional modeling and quantitative rigor; your math ties out and you can defend every claim line by line

 - Strategic counterweight to founders: you push back with conviction, allocate capital intelligently, and think in multi-year arcs while driving near-term velocity

 - High-IQ, high-EQ, high intensity: you process complexity quickly and move fast with sound judgment

 - High Slope: Low ego, high curiosity which is core to our culture. Practical, high-ownership, customer-aware, and exceptionally clear.

 - Bonus: Complex hardware experience. You understand the economics of a hardware-enabled business — installs, logistics, support burden.

 - Bonus: Investment banking or VC experience. You've been a steward of capital: building models that win investors, evaluating allocations, and credibly communicating the books to capital partners.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 1. Own the operating model end to end. Forecast what is likely to happen, and quickly understand when reality diverges from that forecast. Build the drivers, scenario logic, and planning cadence. Be able to tear down and rebuild the model quickly.

 2. Know what is happening in the business — fast, and with high confidence. Command unit economics, contribution margin, and payments economics across a complex business (grocery + hardware + AI + payments), with enough accounting fluency to trust the actuals.

 3. Co-lead the raise. Ensure Vori is an elite steward of others' capital. Design the forward narrative, metrics, and capital structure for the Series C and beyond to IPO. Own the board financial package and represent the company credibly to the board, investors, and capital partners (both equity and debt).

 4. Own the financial foundation. Treat our financial

notes: Vori is a fintech + vertical AI startup transforming the grocery industry. The job board shows a mix of tech, sales, and support roles.
